item.title: How to Grow and Care for Creeping Juniper
item.description:
For a truly carefree, evergreen ground cover, check out creeping juniper. These plants are perfect for rock gardens or xeriscaping and they come in a wide range of options, including dwarf or full-size with green, yellow, gold, or blue foliage. Learn how to choose, plant, and care for these elegant little creepers.

The post How to Grow and Care for Creeping Juniper appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: How to Grow and Care for Apricot Trees
item.description:
Have you ever wanted to grow your own apricot trees? What better time than now? Growing these stone fruits at home means a rare treat: apricots that you can eat the same day you pick them. item.title: 21 of the Best Plants to Grow on a Septic Discharge Field
item.description:

item.title: How to Grow and Care for Creeping Phlox
item.description:
Creeping phlox is a low-maintenance, easygoing perennial that provides perfect ground cover, with stunning flowers in shades of pink, purple, white, and blue. item.title: How to Grow and Care for Baboon Flowers (Babiana)
item.description:
Baboon flowers (Babiana nervosa) are spring-blooming South African natives with spikes of fragrant, funnel-shaped flowers in shades of blue, purple, and yellow. These citrus-scented cormous perennials are ideal for rockeries, cottage gardens, and containers. Learn how to grow baboon flowers in this guide. Read more now.

The post How to Grow and Care for Baboon Flowers (Babiana) appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Learn How to Grow and Care for Gryphon Begonias
item.description:
Gryphon begonia is a shade-loving tropical hybrid prized for its glossy, silver-streaked green foliage with red undersides. The strikingly variegated leaves measure up to 10 inches across for a substantial annual, houseplant, or Zone 8 to 10 perennial. Learn how to grow and care for showy Gryphon begonia in this guide.

The post Learn How to Grow and Care for Gryphon Begonias appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Learn How to Grow Creeping Jenny (Moneywort)
item.description:
Creeping Jenny aka moneywort is a fast-growing, colorful, and lush ground cover in areas with moist soil such as bog margins, creek banks, and pond fringes. Its spread can become aggressive if left unchecked, but its a charming addition to pots and planters. Learn how to grow creeping Jenny in this guide. Read more now.

The post Learn How to Grow Creeping Jenny (Moneywort) appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Learn How to Grow and Care for Asparagus Ferns
item.description:

item.title: How to Grow Dandelions for Greens, Roots, and Flowers
item.description:
You may think of dandelions as invasive weeds, but did you know that the greens, flowers, and roots are edible? Delicious and nutritious, leaves can be added to salads, flowers made into a tea, and the roots can be used as a coffee substitute. Learn how to cultivate and harvest dandelions in this guide. Read more now.

The post How to Grow Dandelions for Greens, Roots, and Flowers appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Tips for Companion Planting in Your Herb Garden
item.description:
Herbs make powerful companion plants, and not just in your vegetable patch. Learn which ones do well planted near each other, and which don't enjoy each other's company.

item.title: How to Grow Chocolate Cosmos Flowers
item.description:
Chocolate cosmos flowers have velvety maroon petals that infuse late-day summer sunshine with the fragrance of chocolate. The species grows as a perennial in Zones 9 to 11 and can be enjoyed as an annual in cooler regions, lifting and storing the tubers at season's end. Learn how to grow chocolate cosmos in this guide.

The post How to Grow Chocolate Cosmos Flowers appeared first on Gardener's Path.
